title: Those scenes which made you feel pity and cry
display_title: Those scenes which made you feel pity and cry
wikitext:
[]: Those scenes which made you feel pity and cry...             Quote Post by Leighton » Thu Mar 10, 2011 9:13 am Just watched EoE again. My eyes watered watching this scene: [URL=http://img651.imageshack.us/i/screenshot20110311at110.png/] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Thu Mar 10, 2011 9:22 am Yeah, the elevator scene in EoE is probably one of my top favorite scenes in all of NGE. It's ball crushingly depressing, and even with my dislike of the English dub I think Allison Keith didn't ruin that scene and actually did a very good job.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Mar 10, 2011 10:04 am None of them. Come on, it's just a cartoon.

[]:             Quote Post by Ritsuko » Thu Mar 10, 2011 10:04 am Everything in EOE :( .

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Mar 10, 2011 11:08 am if anime moves you to tears you're hopeless, sad bastard who doesn't deserve to live

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Mar 10, 2011 11:13 am Komm Susser Tod Xard wrote: if anime moves you to tears you're hopeless, sad bastard who doesn't deserve to live

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Mar 10, 2011 11:15 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Even Grave of the Fireflies?

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Thu Mar 10, 2011 11:19 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Even Grave of the Fireflies?

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Thu Mar 10, 2011 11:55 am Xard wrote: if anime moves you to tears you're hopeless, sad bastard who doesn't deserve to live

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Mar 10, 2011 12:37 pm ITT, Shogo-kun just got Xarded (it's like being trolled, but at the hands of Xard). Xard wrote: Crying at cartoon reveals infantile mind

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Mar 10, 2011 12:58 pm ^ For the record I don't think any anime has made me cry. Some have come close, though.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Mar 10, 2011 3:49 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Even Grave of the Fireflies?

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Thu Mar 10, 2011 4:21 pm BrikHaus wrote: Especially Grave of the Fireflies. Such overrated garbage.

[]:             Quote Post by bigbadtyrant » Thu Mar 10, 2011 4:50 pm The end credits of EOE made me cry because i thought it was over, but then i heard about Rebuild and i beat myself up for crying over that.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Mar 10, 2011 4:53 pm bigbadtyrant wrote: The end credits of EOE made me cry because i thought it was over, but then i heard about Rebuild and i beat myself up for crying over that.

[]:             Quote Post by bigbadtyrant » Thu Mar 10, 2011 4:58 pm Xard wrote: But EoE's ending credits are in middle of film!

[]:             Quote Post by LoCooLaidman » Thu Mar 10, 2011 5:48 pm Err, none! I've never been moved to tears but have been moved to FEEL some sort of emotion for them, Shinji and Lucy(EL) are the only two I can think of.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Mar 10, 2011 6:02 pm Nothing made me teary-eyed in NGE -- it was far too unsentimental for that. Great rushes of pity, yes -- feeling my heart go out to Asuka pretty much constantly from episode 16 on -- but dry eyed. It wasn't until later when I was trying to achieve some catharsis after viewing by writing fanfics that I had to take snuffle breaks (and still do on rereads), It's other silly things that set me off in the moment -- following the reporter's cue in Millennium Actress ; the obviously sentimental Darwin the Dingo episode of Letter Bee ; or the downright weird like the bit in Nanoha A's where diagnostic scans on Bardiche and Raging Heart both report "please install cartridge system add-on".

[]:             Quote Post by Spud » Thu Mar 10, 2011 6:41 pm Well I haven't cried from an anime but one scene did move me It was episode 22 (of coarse) and it's had a deep impact on me. In showing Asuka's character flaws I saw my own (not the same but still flaws), so I decided to change it. But that's where the mind fuck lies for me, realizing in about 15 minutes that you are everything you hated. The series has changed me, for the better. I have changed the way I deal with others, the way I approach problems, my patience , EVERYTHING. Also Asuka's final fight was a little revolting. When it started with her on the bottom of the lake saying over and over, "I don't want to die." In the English dub it actually freaked me out. The furry and hatred mixed with the violence made this an amazing scene, EoE Spoiler SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu Mar 10, 2011 7:43 pm toe mash wrote: Yeah, the elevator scene in EoE is probably one of my top favorite scenes in all of NGE. It's ball crushingly depressing, and even with my dislike of the English dub I think Allison Keith didn't ruin that scene and actually did a very good job.
